name: Grandma's Pumpkin Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine 1 cup Graham cracker crumbs, 1 tablespoon sugar, and 4 tablespoons melted butter (or margarine).
2. Press the mixture into the bottom of a 9-inch spring form pan; chill.
3. In a large mixing bowl, beat together 16 ounces softened cream cheese and 3/4 cup sugar until well blended.
4. Add 1 can pumpkin to the bowl with the cream cheese and mix in 1 1/4 teaspoons ground cinnamon, 1/2 teaspoon ground ginger, 1/2 teaspoon ground nutmeg, and 1/4 teaspoon salt.
5. Beat in 2 eggs one at a time, stirring well after each addition.
6. Pour the filling into the chilled crust and bake at 350 degrees Fahrenheit for 50 minutes.
7. Meanwhile, for topping, combine 2 cups sour cream, 2 tablespoons sugar, and 1 teaspoon vanilla extract.
8. Spread the sour cream mixture over the cheesecake filling; return to the oven for an additional 5 minutes.
9. Cool on a rack and chill overnight.
10. Garnish each slice with a pecan half before serving.
